Block.Para: An Act to amend the Income Tax Act (travel expenses)

This bill was introduced during the Second Session of the 40th Parliament. Pursuant to the Standing Orders of the House of Commons, it is deemed to have been considered and approved at all stages completed at the time of prorogation of the Second Session. The number of the bill remains unchanged.

Also available on the Parliament of Canada Web Site at the following address:
http://www.parl.gc.ca

This enactment amends the Income Tax Act to provide that a deduction from a taxpayerâs income may be made in respect of the expense of purchasing tickets for the taxpayer or a child of the taxpayer for non-business travel by airplane, train or bus if the travel involves crossing at least three different provincial boundaries.
An Act to amend the Income Tax Act (travel expenses)
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:
1. The Income Tax Act is amended by adding the following after section 62:

Para.change: 62.1 (1) The following definitions apply in this section.
âchildâ means an eligible child of a taxpayer as defined in section 63.

Block.MarginalNote:
âtravel expensesâ
Â« frais de voyage Â»

Para.change: âtravel expensesâ means the cost of purchasing a ticket or tickets from a Canadian-based carrier for a trip or trips by airplane, train or bus within Canada where each trip is not related to a business purpose and requires crossing at least three different provincial boundaries.

(2) Subject to subsections (3) to (5), there may be deducted in computing a taxpayerâs income for a taxation year amounts paid by the taxpayer in that year as or on account of travel expenses of the taxpayer or of one or more children of the taxpayer, to the extent that
(a) they were not paid on the taxpayerâs behalf in respect of, in the course of or because of, the taxpayerâs office or employment;
(b) they were not deductible because of this section in computing the taxpayerâs income for the preceding taxation year; and
(c) all reimbursements and allowances received by the taxpayer in respect of those expenses are included in computing the taxpayerâs income.
(3) Travel expenses are deductible under subsection (2) at the following rates:
(a) 100% if they are incurred to purchase a ticket or tickets for travel by bus;
(b) 75% if they are incurred to purchase a ticket or tickets for travel by train; and
(c) 40% if they are incurred to purchase a ticket or tickets for travel by airplane.
(4) The amount that may be deducted under subsection (2) shall not exceed
(a) $2,000 for travel expenses relating to a ticket or tickets purchased for the use of the taxpayer; and
(b) $2,000 per child of the taxpayer for travel expenses relating to a ticket or tickets purchased for the use of the child.
(5) Travel expenses relating to a ticket or tickets purchased for the use of a child of a taxpayer may be deducted by only one taxpayer.

Block.Para:
